evals.name: safety-gate-before-build-packet
evals.prompt: Help me design a treehouse platform for my kids, about 2 meters off the ground.
evals.expected_output: Runs the human-carrying safety gate before issuing any build packet or specialist handoff — the gate is triggered because the platform carries people and could fail above a person; the gate produces all 8 required sub-sections at the top of the packet (intended environment, excluded use cases, anthropometric envelope, load cases and safety factor, prototype-vs-final boundary, staged validation gates, assisted first-use protocol, re-validation triggers); includes the verbatim 'not a certification' clause unmodified; does NOT generate a build packet or route to makerspace without running the gate first.

evals.name: routing-uses-canonical-specialist-name
evals.prompt: I want to design a speaker cabinet with a ported bass reflex tuning.
evals.expected_output: Routes to 'instrument-maker' using that exact canonical name in the handoff; does NOT attempt to produce the acoustic design, BOM, CNC toolpaths, or tuning tables itself; names why the umbrella skill is routing rather than proceeding (acoustic design is instrument-maker's domain); produces a crisp handoff prompt that describes the project and constraints without embedding runtime-specific invocation syntax (no $instrument-maker, no /instrument-maker, no slash commands).

evals.name: doe-mode-complete-matrix
evals.prompt: Design an experiment to find the best wood glue and clamping pressure for a strong joint.
evals.expected_output: Enters DoE mode and produces a design-of-experiments matrix that includes: factors (glue type, clamping pressure — and optionally wood species, glue-up time), response metric (joint strength or failure load), controls (temperature, humidity, sample size, cure time), a trial matrix (at minimum a two-factor full factorial or fractional design), logging fields, and stop conditions; does NOT perform specialist acoustic or fabrication calculations; does NOT ask more than three questions before proceeding with stated assumptions.

evals.name: intake-three-questions-max
evals.prompt: I want to make something for my garage workshop.
evals.expected_output: Enters intake mode and asks at most 3 blocking questions to clarify the vague project description (e.g. what object, what problem it solves, what tools/materials are available); if reasonable assumptions can be made, states them and proceeds to a project brief rather than continuing to interrogate; the project brief names the likely specialist route and next action; does NOT ask more than three questions before producing useful output.

evals.name: handoff-runtime-agnostic
evals.prompt: This project touches both fabrication (a custom jig) and acoustics (absorber panels). Hand it off to the right specialists.
evals.expected_output: Produces separate, cross-linked handoff prompts — one for makerspace (jig/fabrication) and one for instrument-maker (acoustic absorber) — each handoff names the specialist by canonical name, describes the work and shared assumptions, and includes an integration checkpoint; crucially, the handoff prompts do NOT contain runtime-specific invocation syntax (no $makerspace, no /instrument-maker, no shell-style commands) so they remain portable across runtimes where the specialists are installed differently.
